results.title: Cochin Shipyard shares fall 3% amid buzz around OFS at 8% discount
results.description: Cochin Shipyard shares dipped Monday following reports of a potential government Offer for Sale (OFS) at a discount. The government, aiming to raise funds, is reportedly planning to sell a stake soon, adding to recent disinvestment activities in other public sector undertakings. Despite a recent stock decline, the company has shown strong long-term returns and improved operational efficiency in its latest earnings report.

results.title: Iran war restarts? Massive explosion rocks tUS Arab ally’s energy heartland
results.description: A massive explosion and fire at Qatar’s Barzan gas processing facility sparked speculation across social media, with dramatic images showing a large fireball and smoke rising above one of the Gulf’s most important energy hubs. Qatari authorities and QatarEnergy said the incident was caused by an industrial malfunction during operational procedures and not by hostile action. Several injuries were reported, but officials stressed there was no threat from hazardous emissions. The blast occurred at a sensitive moment as U.S.-Iran negotiations continue and regional tensions remain elevated around key energy infrastructure.

results.title: Sanjay Leela Bhansali’s Love & War Hit by Tragedy; Crew Member Dies, Ranbir-Alia-Vicky Film Halts
results.description: Love & War Crew Member Dies After Electrocution, FWICE Seeks Rs. 50 Lakh Compensation The production of Love & War, Sanjay Leela Bhansali's much-awaited period drama starring Ranbir Kapoor, Alia Bhatt, and Vicky Kaushal, has seen a tragic halt. A crew member reportedly died while working on the film’s set recently and the accident has sparked a safety alarm in Bollywood. According to reports, Chandradhari Singh Yadav, a carpenter employed on the film's set, suffered an electric shock during work and died in the early hours of June 17. The incident reportedly occurred around 3 am. FWICE Demands Increased Compensation Following the incident, the Federation of Western India Cine Employees (FWICE) has sought enhanced compensation and long-term support for the deceased worker's family. FWICE President BN Tiwari said the federation has requested Bhansali's production house to increase the compensation amount from Rs. 40 lakh to Rs. 50 lakh. “Firstly, we want the compensation to be Rs. 50 lakh given that his children are very young and the family will need aid for their education,” Tiwari said. FWICE has also urged the production company to bear the educational expenses of Chandradhari's two children. In addition, the union has requested employment for the widow of the deceased worker to provide a steady stream of income for the family. Concerns Arising Over Work Conditions This case has once again raised concerns about working conditions on film sets. As per the report in Moneycontrol , FWICE General Secretary Ashok Dubey said that Chandradhari was working for very long hours prior to this unfortunate event. He was working from 7 am till 3 am the next morning and this continued for three days at a stretch. Tiwari stressed the requirement of more safety measures on film sets and the reduction of working hours for technicians and crew members. "It is tragic that he died in this manner because he was such a gifted technician," he said. About Love & War Love & War is one of the most anticipated Hindi films currently in production. The film marks Sanjay Leela Bhansali's first collaboration featuring Ranbir Kapoor, Alia Bhatt and Vicky Kaushal together. As per reports, the upcoming drama film will center around a complicated love story between two police officers, featuring Ranbir Kapoor and Vicky Kaushal, along with Alia Bhatt , who is caught between her professional and personal life. The film is also the first collaboration between Ranbir and Alia on screen since Brahmastra: Part One: Shiva in 2022. Whereas for Vicky Kaushal, he is collaborating again with both co-stars since Sanju and Raazi, respectively.
